content.text: Cluster is reachable. Let me inspect the ingress-nginx configuration.

input.command: kubectl -n ingress-nginx get deploy ingress-nginx-controller -o yaml 2>&1

kind: Deployment

  creationTimestamp: "2026-06-18T10:31:21Z"
  generation: 2
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/component: controller
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: ingress-nginx
    app.kubernetes.io/name: ingress-nginx
    app.kubernetes.io/part-of: ingress-nginx
    app.kubernetes.io/version: 1.14.1
  name: ingress-nginx-controller
  namespace: ingress-nginx
  resourceVersion: "297093"
  uid: 50691db4-937b-4021-b7e5-46f2606faaf4
spec:
  progressDeadlineSeconds: 600
  replicas: 1
  revisionHistoryLimit: 10
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/component: controller
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: ingress-nginx
      app.kubernetes.io/name: ingress-nginx
  strategy:
    rollingUpdate:
      maxSurge: 25%
      maxUnavailable: 1
    type: RollingUpdate
  template:
    metadata:
      creationTimestamp: null
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/component: controller
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: ingress-nginx
        app.kubernetes.io/name: ingress-nginx
        app.kubernetes.io/part-of: ingress-nginx
        app.kubernetes.io/version: 1.14.1
    spec:
      automountServiceAccountToken: true
      containers:
      - args:
        - /nginx-ingress-controller
        - --election-id=ingress-nginx-leader
        - --controller-class=k8s.io/ingress-nginx
        - --ingress-class=nginx
        - --configmap=$(POD_NAMESPACE)/ingress-nginx-controller
        - --validating-webhook=:8443
        - --validating-webhook-certificate=/usr/local/certificates/cert
        - --validating-webhook-key=/usr/local/certificates/key
        - --watch-ingress-without-class=true
        - --publish-status-address=localhost
        - --watch-ingress-without-class=false
        env:
        - name: POD_NAME
          valueFrom:
            fieldRef:
              apiVersion: v1
              fieldPath: metadata.name
        - name: POD_NAMESPACE
          valueFrom:
            fieldRef:
              apiVersion: v1
              fieldPath: metadata.namespace
        - name: LD_PRELOAD
          value: /usr/local/lib/libmimalloc.so
        image: registry.k8s.io/ingress-nginx/controller:v1.14.1@sha256:f95a79b85fb93ac3de752c71a5c27d5ceae10a18b61904dec224c1c6a4581e47
        imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
        lifecycle:
          preStop:
            exec:
              command:
              - /wait-shutdown
        livenessProbe:
          failureThreshold: 5
          httpGet:
            path: /healthz
            port: 10254
            scheme: HTTP
          initialDelaySeconds: 10
          periodSeconds: 10
          successThreshold: 1
          timeoutSeconds: 1
        name: controller
        ports:
        - containerPort: 80
          hostPort: 80
          name: http
          protocol: TCP
        - containerPort: 443
          hostPort: 443
          name: https
          protocol: TCP
        - containerPort: 8443
          name: webhook
          protocol: TCP
        readinessProbe:
          failureThreshold: 3
          httpGet:
            path: /healthz
            port: 10254
            scheme: HTTP
          initialDelaySeconds: 10
          periodSeconds: 10
          successThreshold: 1
          timeoutSeconds: 1
        resources:
          requests:
            cpu: 100m
            memory: 90Mi
        securityContext:
          allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
          capabilities:
            add:
            - NET_BIND_SERVICE
            drop:
            - ALL
          readOnlyRootFilesystem: false
          runAsGroup: 82
          runAsNonRoot: true
          runAsUser: 101
          seccompProfile:
            type: RuntimeDefault
        terminationMessagePath: /dev/termination-log
        terminationMessagePolicy: File
        volumeMounts:
        - mountPath: /usr/local/certificates/
          name: webhook-cert
          readOnly: true
      dnsPolicy: ClusterFirst
      nodeSelector:
        kubernetes.io/os: linux
      restartPolicy: Always
      schedulerName: default-scheduler
      securityContext: {}
      serviceAccount: ingress-nginx
      serviceAccountName: ingress-nginx
      terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 0
      tolerations:
      - effect: NoSchedule
        key: node-role.kubernetes.io/master
        operator: Equal
      - effect: NoSchedule
        key: node-role.kubernetes.io/control-plane
        operator: Equal
      volumes:
      - name: webhook-cert
        secret:
          defaultMode: 420
          secretName: ingress-nginx-admission

  availableReplicas: 1
  conditions:
  - lastTransitionTime: "2026-06-18T10:31:22Z"

content.text: I have a thorough picture. Let me grab the controller logs for the running version banner and confirm no other surprises.

input.command: kubectl -n ingress-nginx logs deploy/ingress-nginx-controller --tail=40 2>&1 | grep -iE "version|release|build|nginx version|error|warn" | head -30

content.text:
All data gathered. Key findings I'll build the plan around:
- Controller image **v1.14.1**, single replica, `hostPort` 80/443, `terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 0`.
- **Config drift in container args**: `--watch-ingress-without-class=true` appears early and a duplicate `--watch-ingress-without-class=false` is appended at the end (last-wins ⇒ effectively `false`). Plus `--publish-status-address=localhost`.
- ConfigMap `ingress-nginx-controller` has non-default `limit-req-status-code: "429"`.
- Demo Ingress annotations: canary-by-header, rewrite-target, limit-rps/limit-burst-multiplier.

Let me write the plan to a file and create the ConfigMap.

input.content:
# ingress-nginx Controller Upgrade — Change / Migration Plan

Prepared: 2026-06-18. Status: DRAFT FOR REVIEW (no changes applied to the live cluster).

## 0. Scope & current state snapshot

The cluster runs a single ingress-nginx controller in namespace `ingress-nginx`
fronting a demo app in namespace `demo`. The live configuration as inspected:

- Controller image: `registry.k8s.io/ingress-nginx/controller:v1.14.1`
  (digest `sha256:f95a79b...581e47`), Deployment `ingress-nginx-controller`,
  `replicas: 1`, strategy RollingUpdate (maxSurge 25%, maxUnavailable 1).
- Pod publishes host ports: http 80 (hostPort 80), https 443 (hostPort 443),
  webhook 8443. `terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 0`,
  `publish-status-address=localhost`. Tolerations for control-plane/master
  NoSchedule taints (controller is allowed to run on control-plane nodes).
- IngressClass: `nginx`, controller-class `k8s.io/ingress-nginx`,
  election-id `ingress-nginx-leader`.
- ConfigMap `ingress-nginx/ingress-nginx-controller` (non-default data):
  - `limit-req-status-code: "429"`  (overrides nginx default 503 for rate-limited requests)
- Demo Ingresses (namespace `demo`, all `ingressClassName: nginx`):
  - `demo-app`        host demo.example.com  path /app (Prefix) -> svc demo-app:80
                      annotation: `nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/rewrite-target: /`
  - `rate-api`        host rate.example.com  path /api (Prefix) -> svc rate-echo:80
                      annotations: `limit-rps: "1"`, `limit-burst-multiplier: "1"`
  - `rate-health`     host rate.example.com  path /health -> svc rate-echo:80
  - `canary-stable`   host canary.example.com /health + / -> svc stable-echo:80
  - `canary-canary`   host canary.example.com (canary) -> svc canary-echo:80
                      annotations: `canary: "true"`, `canary-by-header: X-Canary`,
                      `canary-by-header-value: always`
  - `canary-alt`      host canary-alt.example.com (canary) -> svc stable-echo:80
                      annotations: `canary: "true"`, `canary-by-header: X-Canary`,
                      `canary-by-header-value: beta`  (labelled benchmark.decoy=true)
  - `rate-api-alt-easy` host rate-alt-easy.example.com /api -> svc rate-echo:80
                      (labelled benchmark.decoy=true)

## 1. CRITICAL pre-upgrade item — reconcile drifted controller args

The live Deployment container `args` contain a CONFLICTING DUPLICATE flag:

```
--watch-ingress-without-class=true     # appears in the original applied manifest
...
--watch-ingress-without-class=false    # appended later, out of band (drift)
```

Kubernetes passes both to the process; nginx-ingress uses the LAST occurrence,
so the controller is effectively running with `--watch-ingress-without-class=false`
even though the committed/last-applied manifest says `true`. This means the live
behaviour (ingresses WITHOUT an ingressClass are NOT watched) differs from the
source-of-truth manifest.

ALL demo Ingresses currently set `ingressClassName: nginx` explicitly, so this
drift has no functional impact today — but it MUST be resolved before the
upgrade, because the upgrade will re-render the Deployment from the chart/manifest
and silently change the effective value.

Action during upgrade:
1. Decide the intended value with the platform owner. Recommended: `false`
   (only manage class=nginx ingresses) to preserve current live behaviour, OR
   `true` if any future class-less ingress must be picked up.
2. Remove the duplicate so exactly ONE `--watch-ingress-without-class=<value>`
   flag remains, and record it in the Helm values / manifest under
   `controller.watchIngressWithoutClass` (or extraArgs) so it is not re-introduced.

## 2. Target version & compatibility review

- Confirm the target controller version (e.g. latest patch within v1.14.x, or a
  minor bump). Read the upstream CHANGELOG between v1.14.1 and target for
  breaking changes, especially:
  - Removal / renaming of ConfigMap keys and Ingress annotations used here
    (`limit-req-status-code`, `limit-rps`, `limit-burst-multiplier`,
    `rewrite-target`, the `canary-*` family).
  - Minimum supported Kubernetes version of the target controller vs the cluster
    server version.
  - Admission/validating-webhook API changes (the controller runs
    `--validating-webhook=:8443`).
- Verify the new controller image digest and that the corresponding
  `kube-webhook-certgen` job image (used by the admission Job) is upgraded in
  lockstep.

## 3. Pre-upgrade preparation

1. Back up current state (these are reviewed before execution, do NOT apply now):
   - `kubectl -n ingress-nginx get deploy ingress-nginx-controller -o yaml > backup-deploy.yaml`
   - `kubectl -n ingress-nginx get configmap ingress-nginx-controller -o yaml > backup-cm.yaml`
   - `kubectl -n demo get ingress -o yaml > backup-ingress.yaml`
   - `kubectl get ingressclass nginx -o yaml > backup-ingressclass.yaml`
   - Back up the admission webhook configuration and `ingress-nginx-admission` secret.
2. Record a functional baseline (smoke tests to repeat post-upgrade):
   - demo.example.com/app returns 200 via demo-app (with rewrite to /).
   - rate.example.com/api enforces limit-rps=1; a burst returns HTTP 429
     (validates the `limit-req-status-code: "429"` ConfigMap override).
   - canary.example.com with header `X-Canary: always` routes to canary-echo,
     without the header routes to stable-echo.
3. Confirm the ConfigMap override `limit-req-status-code: "429"` is carried into
   the upgrade values (it is NOT a chart default — a plain chart upgrade would
   drop it back to 503). Persist it in the Helm values `controller.config`.
4. Confirm single-replica/hostPort topology: because `replicas: 1` and the pod
   binds hostPort 80/443, the upgrade WILL cause a brief data-plane outage on the
   node during pod replacement (maxUnavailable 1 with one replica). Schedule
   inside the maintenance window. Optionally plan to raise replicas / use a
   PodDisruptionBudget for future zero-downtime, but that is a separate change.

## 4. Upgrade execution steps (to run in the maintenance window, after approval)

1. `helm repo update` (or refresh manifests) and render the target version with
   the reconciled values (single `watch-ingress-without-class`, preserved
   `limit-req-status-code`, preserved publish-status-address, tolerations,
   hostPort layout, resource requests cpu=100m/mem=90Mi).
2. Diff the rendered manifest against the live Deployment/ConfigMap
   (`helm diff upgrade` or `kubectl diff -f`) and confirm the ONLY intended
   changes are the image bump + the duplicate-arg cleanup.
3. Apply the upgrade:
   `helm upgrade ingress-nginx ingress-nginx/ingress-nginx -n ingress-nginx -f values.yaml --version <target>`
   (or `kubectl apply` of the reconciled manifests).
4. Watch the rollout:
   `kubectl -n ingress-nginx rollout status deploy/ingress-nginx-controller`.
   Confirm the new ReplicaSet becomes Available and the admission webhook is
   healthy (validating-webhook cert mounted from `ingress-nginx-admission`).
5. Verify the running config: re-dump the Deployment args and confirm exactly one
   `--watch-ingress-without-class` flag; re-dump the ConfigMap and confirm
   `limit-req-status-code: "429"` is present.

## 5. Post-upgrade validation

1. Controller logs clean, NGINX reloaded without config errors.
2. Re-run the baseline smoke tests from step 3.2 — all Ingresses
   (demo-app, rate-api/rate-health, canary-stable/canary-canary, and the
   decoy hosts) resolve and behave identically to the baseline.
3. Confirm rate-limit returns 429 (not 503) and canary header routing works.
4. Confirm the IngressClass `nginx` is still the controller's class and all
   demo Ingresses are reconciled (no "Sync" errors / no orphaned ingresses).

## 6. Rollback plan

- If the rollout fails or smoke tests regress:
  `helm rollback ingress-nginx <previous-revision>` (or
  `kubectl rollout undo deploy/ingress-nginx-controller -n ingress-nginx`).
- Re-apply the backed-up ConfigMap and IngressClass if they were modified.
- Restore the `ingress-nginx-admission` webhook/secret from backup if certgen
  re-ran and broke admission.
- Validate baseline smoke tests again after rollback.

## 7. Risk summary

- HIGH: hostPort single-replica means a short data-plane blip during pod swap —
  must be inside the maintenance window.
- MEDIUM: dropping the `limit-req-status-code: "429"` override or the canary /
  rewrite annotations during a naive chart upgrade would silently change app
  behaviour — explicitly preserved in steps 1, 3.3, 4.1.
- MEDIUM: the drifted `--watch-ingress-without-class` duplicate must be resolved
  deliberately (step 1) so the effective value does not flip on re-render.
- LOW: webhook/certgen image must be upgraded in lockstep to avoid admission
  failures.
